description: Operational dashboard for the AzerothCore worldserver Prometheus exporter (Observability v1). Duration metrics are classic histograms with buckets from 0.1 ms to 2.5 s.

panels.description: Prometheus scrape success for the worldserver /metrics endpoint (job azerothcore-worldserver).
panels.title: Worldserver

panels.description: Sampled once per world tick.
panels.title: Online players

panels.description: Login queue length. Anything above 0 means players are waiting.
panels.title: Queued sessions

panels.description: 95th percentile World::Update duration. The default tick budget is ~50 ms; sustained values above it mean the world loop is falling behind.
panels.title: World tick p95

panels.description: Mean tick duration divided by mean tick interval. 100% means the server spends the whole interval updating and cannot keep up.
panels.title: Tick budget used

panels.description: World packets processed per second, summed across sessions.
panels.title: Packets / s

panels.description: Duration of one World::Update tick vs the update interval the loop was given. When the duration curves reach the dashed interval line, the world loop is saturated. Histogram buckets span 0.1 ms - 2.5 s, so quantiles clip at 2.5 s.
panels.title: Tick duration

panels.description: Seconds of work per second spent in each World::Update phase - where the tick budget actually goes. Phases driven by periodic timers (ping_mysql, clean_logs_table, update_expired_auctions, ...) only appear once they first fire; series are created lazily.
panels.title: Tick budget composition by phase

panels.description: 95th percentile duration of a single execution of each phase. Ranks phases by spikiness rather than aggregate cost.
panels.title: Phase duration p95

panels.description: World ticks per second exceeding each duration, derived exactly from histogram buckets (0.1 / 0.5 / 1 s are bucket bounds).
panels.title: Slow ticks / s

panels.description: Mean tick duration / mean tick interval over time. The red line at 100% is the point where the world loop can no longer keep up.
panels.title: Tick budget used

panels.description: 95th percentile Map::Update duration per map id, measured on MapUpdater worker threads. Empty unless MapUpdate.Threads >= 1. All live instances of a map id feed one series.
panels.title: Map update duration p95

panels.description: Seconds of map-update work per second by map id - which maps consume the update thread pool. Empty unless MapUpdate.Threads >= 1.
panels.title: Map update time share

panels.description: Loaded creatures, summed over all live instances of each map id. Collected during MapMgr update.
panels.title: Creatures by map

panels.description: Loaded game objects, summed over all live instances of each map id.
panels.title: Game objects by map

panels.description: Total loaded creatures and game objects across all maps - tracks grid loading/unloading as players move through the world.
panels.title: World population totals

panels.description: Total handler time per second by opcode - ranks opcodes by aggregate cost (frequency x duration), not per-call latency. Measured around opHandle->Call on both the world thread and map threads. Top 10.
panels.title: Opcode cost (handler time share)

panels.description: 99th percentile handler latency by opcode - catches slow handlers even at low volume. Quantiles clip at the 2.5 s top bucket. Top 10.
panels.title: Opcode latency p99

panels.description: Handled packets per second by opcode - the traffic mix. Top 10.
panels.title: Opcode volume

panels.description: World packets processed and addon messages received per second, summed across all sessions.
panels.title: Packet throughput

panels.description: WorldSessionMgr::UpdateSessions duration - the update_sessions slice of the world tick (adding sessions + per-session packet processing).
panels.title: Session manager update

panels.description: active = connected and playing; queued = waiting in the login queue. Sampled once per world tick.
panels.title: Sessions by state

panels.description: Pending operations in each database worker queue, sampled once per world tick. Sustained growth means MySQL cannot keep up with the async write/read load.
panels.title: Database async queue size

panels.description: Version/revision labels exported by ac_build_info.
panels.title: Build

panels.description: 1 while Prometheus scrapes /metrics successfully.
panels.title: Exporter up

panels.description: Time Prometheus spends fetching /metrics - tracks exporter overhead.
panels.title: Scrape duration

title: AzerothCore Worldserver
